texto: Every woman I'd ever known had two sets of memories: the one they wanted to remember and the one their heart wouldn't let them forget. The first kind were chosen, mostly positive and personality building, but the second would live on forever, despite age and fatigue and life-stealing diseases like dementia and Alzheimer's. Coded on the heart like a hard drive, the feelings never vanished.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Max Monroe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Every woman I'd ever known had two sets of memories: the one they wanted to remember and the one …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, heart, memories pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de memories e women.

The first kind were chosen, mostly positive and personality building, but the second would live on forever, despite age and fatigue and life-stealing diseases like dementia and Alzheimer's. Coded on the heart like a hard drive, the feelings never vanished.

texto: Most times, even if giving your heart away ends in heartbreak, the risk was still worth it. Sometimes, that risk gives you little miracles. and sometimes, that risk gives you life lessons that allow you to grow and learn more about yourself.
explicacao: Citação de Max Monroe sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Most times, even if giving your heart away ends in heartbreak, the risk was still worth it. Somet…” articula reflexao, life-lessons, sometimes em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.
